常見問題：
AC米蘭同國際米蘭又係有"米蘭"，應該點分？
ANS：AC米蘭簡稱米蘭，國際米蘭叫國米，避免稱呼做AC，因為AC係球會咁解，而milan先係名既簡稱。
AC米蘭仲係咪「老人院」？
ANS：現時全隊平均年齡為25歲。
今個轉會窗到目前為止簽咗咁多球員，遲啲仲會唔會有錢買人？
ANS：其實買球員好少會一炮過畀，多數都係分期，所以仲會有錢簽其他球員。
簽咗咁多球員會唔會過唔到FFP？
ANS：早前米蘭管理層有同UEFA探討經營計劃。
http://sempremilan.com/ts-fassone-meets-uefa-to-discuss-ac-milan-business-plan（By 二石四鳥)

Gattuso: "The biggest concern is to underestimate our opponents. My job is to make the players understand that it will not be easy, they have few pacy players. We must not play with fire, they can put us in difficulty."

Gattuso: "At this moment we have to think about tomorrow. In Europe there is the draw component, we must be more lucky in getting less strong or in poor form teams."

Gattuso: "What I expect from the players who play less? I want to see what I touch with my hand and the concepts I work on during the week. I read about Forte and Dias, it's not true, they won't even be called up. We have to respect Ludogorets and field players with experience."

Gattuso: "The first leg result was a deceiving one if we analyze the game. It wasn't a game of high intensity, we always arrived second to the ball. In the first half we were lucky to break the deadlock right before half-time. Ludogorets could've also scored."

Gattuso: "Ludogorets is a team that can play football, we must respect them. If we think that we've already qualified we are wrong, and I am seriously speaking. As a player I was leading 3-0 and you all know how that game ended. We have everything to lose tomorrow."

Gattuso: "Rumors about the ownership disturb me and the atmosphere? No, because the salaries arrive in advance. On organizational level Milan is a perfect machine. When I speak with Fassone and Mirabelli they tell me that the money is there. I touch everything I say."

Gattuso: "The payment of Andre Silva's transfer from Porto to Milan was completed two months before the deadline and the Portuguese club was surprised about this. If there was really no money, how would this be possible?"

Gattuso: "Many times you have to stop him [Andre Silva], he is one of the last players to leave Milanello. The value of Andre Silva is what we saw in the 20 minutes against Sampdoria."

Gattuso: "Silva has changed a lot in months, now he has to find the goal. He is a great player and we are happy with him. We are very close to him, he does not speak a lot but I understand what kind of a guy he is."

Gattuso: "Tomorrow Andre Silva will play for 90 minutes and he must prove what he can do."

Gattuso: "The management has chosen me,they could have chosen coaches with much more experience and importance than me since they had the economic possibility. I would never allow myself to speak about a renewal. I do not even have an agent that follows me, it's not my priority."

Croatian striker Nikola Kalinić has just a few months left in which to convince AC Milan of his worth ahead of the summer transfer window. Since joining the Rossoneri on loan with obligation to buy from Fiorentina last year, but has struggled to establish himself at San Siro due to fitness problems and a lack of consistency on the pitch.

The 30-year-old has found the net just four times in Serie A this season, which is far too little when you consider he was supposed to be the team’s main focal point in attack. That said, he has proved to be a very useful player from a tactical perspective as he can hold the ball up very well and knows how to bring his teammates into play. However, goals have been in short supply and this is why the club may be tempted to cash in on him come the end of the campaign.

Calciomercato.com reporter Daniele Longo understands the player was proposed to Schalke 04 last month, but the Gelsenkirchen-based side were not interested in negotiating a deal to take him to the Bundesliga. On the other hand, Eintracht Frankfurt have indicated that they would be keen to sign him should they defy the odds and qualify for next season’s UEFA Champions League. He is valued at around €35 million, but no decision will be taken on his future until the Milanese giants finalise their plans for next season.

According to Calciomercato.com, Schalke and Frankfurt were both approached by intermediaries representing Kalinic in January.

Although Schalke rejected their advances, the website notes Frankfurt did show an interest.

However, Milan would only be prepared to sell the Croatian for a profit, having agreed to pay Fiorentina €25m for an obligatory purchase option last summer.

The 30-year-old has scored just four goals for the Rossoneri this season, with injury problems limiting his impact.

However, CM notes he has shown “physical improvements” in recent training sessions and could be given a chance to impress against Roma on Sunday.

Frankfurt, for their part, beat RB Leipzig 2-1 on Monday to move up to third in the Bundesliga table.

Kessie: 'Gattuso not like Montella'

\Franck Kessie didn’t hold back on criticism of former Milan Coach Vincenzo Montella. “With Gennaro Gattuso we actually work on tactics.”

The Rossoneri have been completely transformed since Gattuso took over, unbeaten in 12 competitive games and tonight earning a 2-0 victory away to Roma.

“Gattuso is making us work harder, especially on tactics. With Montella we did very little work in training, but it’s different with Gattuso and you can see the results on the pitch,” the midfielder told Mediaset Premium.

“The locker room atmosphere is the same as it always was, the difference is that now we work harder. It’s like a family here and the results are arriving.”

Milan are now joint sixth in Serie A, will play Arsenal in the Europa League Round of 16 and visit Lazio on Wednesday for the Coppa Italia semi-final.

“We are now focusing on the Lazio game, then we’ll think about the rest. One step at a time.”

Meanwhile, Montella's Sevilla side were beaten 5-2 at home by Atletico Madrid this evening.


反蒙勢力之一

Calabria: 'Chip was spontaneous'

Davide Calabria credited Gennaro Gattuso with giving Milan a “winning mentality” and described his sensational debut goal. “The chip was spontaneous.”

The 21-year-old full-back scored the first professional goal of his career and did it with some style, springing the offside trap for a dinked finish over Alisson as Milan won 2-0 away to Roma.

“My teammates complimented me at the end of the game. I’ll have to buy them all dinner!” Calabria told Mediaset Premium and Sky Sport Italia.

“I found myself there in front of Alisson and the chip was just spontaneous. Things are going so well at the moment, it helps confidence.

“We are all going through a great period of form. It was an excellent performance tonight and we are happy to have brought home the three points.

“Gattuso made us understand from the first day what it means to wear this jersey. We are working with great intensity and have to continue along this path.

“The winning mentality that Gattuso brings is incredible, as he focuses on every detail. He is passing that mentality on to us now.

“Every day he tells me that I have the mind of a 40-year-old and the body of one too, as I have to stop often in training!”

Calabria came up through the Milan youth academy and is no stranger to club legends.

“I grew up watching Paolo Maldini, but don’t anybody dare start making comparisons.”

加度素在2-0擊敗羅馬的比賽後談到了球隊的表現：“我喜歡和小伙子們這種融洽的關係。這是一場非常艱苦的比賽，上半場我們遇到了很多困難，下半場我們開始嘗試不同的打法，尋求不一樣的進攻方式。我認為羅馬是一支十分優秀的球隊，想要擊敗他們我也付出了艱苦的努力。”

“我們本賽季確實可以做得更好，包括我剛開始執教球隊的時候也是如此，我們當初還在賓尼雲圖身上失掉了分數。我並不認為球隊的實力發生了變化，重要的是比賽獲勝的決心和團結精神。”

“中場休息後發生了什麼變化？上半場我們傳出的球很容易被對手判斷，我不喜歡這樣。我更願意讓邊鋒內收，讓邊後衛助攻上去。基斯爾助攻了卡拉比拿的入球，他幫助卡拉比拿創造了進攻空間。無論有球還是無球狀態下，我都喜歡球員們多多跑動。我也想稱讚球隊的防守，球員們總是在正確的時間像整體一樣跑動，對此我非常開心。”

“週三我們有一場非常重要的比賽，我們真正關心的就是能否進入意大利盃決賽。之後我們會再考慮和國際米蘭以及阿仙奴的較量。”

“今天阿仙奴輸球了，但我們不是曼城，我們必須為這場戰鬥做好充分準備。”

依邊 Davide Calabria credited Gennaro Gattuso with giving Milan a “winning mentality”

另一邊, Montella:
"A team like ours can't afford to lose confidence,"

"Defeats like today are always annoying, our heads went down at 2-0."We must improve on that during matches."

"It hasn't been a good night,"

"We have to analyse this performance because there are so many elements which needed to be addressed."

"The first half an hour was the best since I've been here, but we didn't score."
